routes._comment: Homepage at /home/ (the bare /  root redirects to /home/). RoTLD is the .ro TLD registry, niche but every developer/lawyer/journalist hits it (per task spec). Bootstrap 3 site with bespoke .rt-* prefixed classes (.rt-navbar, .rt-nav, .rt-whois-input, .rt-navbar-brand) — these are stable, owned by the registry's own theme. Three primary widgets on the homepage: a hero h1, a 3-card service grid (Administrare Domenii / Formulare / Adrese IP), and the WHOIS lookup form. Selector source: live inspection of https://www.rotld.ro/home/ on 2026-05-02 via Playwright.

extract._comment: Hero title — the only h1 on the page, with class .highlight (bespoke RoTLD class).
Welcome subtitle — first h3.highlight after the hero h1.
Site-wide nav — .rt-nav is the bespoke nav class. We collect the top-level <a> children only.
Brand link — .rt-navbar-brand is the logo anchor.
WHOIS lookup form — the page's primary action. Posts GET to /whois. The input has class .rt-whois-input and name="domain".
Service cards — the homepage 3-card grid (Administrare Domenii, Formulare, Adrese IP). Each is wrapped in <div class="col-xs-12 col-sm-12 col-md-4 text-center"> with an <h3> inside.
Service-card 'Mai multe' anchors — every card ends with a 'Mai multe' link to its destination.
Cookie banner — the cookieconsent library's banner with class .cc-window. The replacement consent module proxies to .cc-btn.cc-dismiss.

pensioner._comment: Pensioner — RoTLD is technical by nature, but the pensioner overlay still shows the page-title, the WHOIS form (one input, one button), and the three service cards as one-tap-per-row tiles. Hide nav and cookie banner.

pro._comment: Pro persona (developer / sysadmin) — the WHOIS form and the deep-nav matter most. Surface all the nav items.

journalist._comment: Journalist — wants WHOIS for source verification, plus the service catalogue.
